output.summary: Four changes requested: (1) Update test page with DemoContainer wrappers, missing variations (long content, empty optional, eyebrow), and interactive tests; (2) Expand accessibility requirements in the spec and QA checks (landmark roles, aria-live announcements, keyboard navigation for filters/pagination, focus management after AJAX, disabled state on Load More); (3) Fix missing hover effect on policy and resource cards (policy btn-tertiary not changing to charcoal on hover, resource/policy tag borders not inverting on hover); (4) Blog post card hover should trigger image zoom AND Learn More accent color simultaneously when the link element is hovered (currently the CTA hover is only on .card-post-cta:hover, not on the parent group hover).

fileChanges.description: Add group-hover:text-charcoal! to btn-tertiary span so the CTA text changes color when the card is hovered
fileChanges.details: Line 29: the <span class="btn-tertiary"> needs group-hover:text-charcoal! added (matching card-resource.php line 46). Also add group-hover classes to the tag pills so their bg/border invert on hover (currently tags stay accent-lighter on the accent hover background, making them nearly invisible).

fileChanges.description: Add group-hover classes to tag pills so they invert properly on card hover
fileChanges.details: Tag pills (lines 20, 29) use bg-accent-lighter border-accent-light which blends into the accent hover background. Add group-hover:bg-charcoal/10 group-hover:border-charcoal/20 to make tags visible on hover. Also ensure the arrow SVG inside btn-tertiary gets the charcoal color on hover.

fileChanges.description: Move the Learn More accent-color hover from .card-post-cta:hover to group-hover so it triggers when any part of the card link is hovered, and add image scale on hover
fileChanges.details: Currently the accent color on Learn More only shows when hovering directly on the CTA element (Archive.css line 111-114 targets .card-post-cta:hover). User wants the image zoom and Learn More accent color to animate simultaneously when hovering anywhere on the card link. Fix: (1) Add group-hover:scale-105 transition-transform duration-300 to the post thumbnail img element. (2) Change CSS from .card-post-cta:hover to .card-post .group:hover .card-post-cta (or use group-hover Tailwind classes on the span). Both the image animation and CTA color change should fire on the parent <a class="group"> hover.

fileChanges.description: Update the blog card CTA hover rule to use group hover instead of direct element hover, and add image hover transition
fileChanges.details: Change line 111-114 from targeting .card-post-cta:hover/.card-post-cta:focus to targeting .card-post .group:hover .card-post-cta and .card-post .group:focus-within .card-post-cta. This ensures hovering anywhere on the card link triggers the accent color on Learn More. Add a rule for .card-post .group:hover img { transform: scale(1.05); } with transition on the img element. Also add .archive .card-policy .group:hover .btn-tertiary { color: var(--color-charcoal); } for the policy card btn-tertiary fix (in case Tailwind group-hover is not sufficient due to specificity with the btn-tertiary component).

fileChanges.description: Add accessibility improvements: aria-live region enhancements, focus management, disabled state announcements, and landmark roles
fileChanges.details: 1. Add aria-busy="true" to .archive-results during AJAX loads (managed via JS). 2. Add role="status" to loading spinner with aria-label. 3. Ensure the filter form has role="search" when search is enabled (currently role="form"). 4. Add aria-label to the per-page selector. 5. Add aria-disabled and aria-label to the Load More button when it's loading. 6. The section already has the filter form with role="form" and aria-label, and results with aria-live="polite" -- these are good. Verify the Clear All button has an aria-label.

fileChanges.description: Add aria-busy toggling during AJAX loads, focus management after content updates, and disabled state management
fileChanges.details: 1. Set aria-busy="true" on results container when AJAX starts, aria-busy="false" when done. 2. After AJAX load (non-append), move focus to the results container or first result card for screen reader awareness. 3. When Load More is clicked, set aria-disabled="true" and aria-busy="true", restore when complete. 4. Add keyboard event handlers for filter pill removal (Enter/Space). 5. Announce filter changes to screen readers by updating a live region.

fileChanges.description: Rebuild the test page with DemoContainer wrappers, missing variations, and proper interactive test definitions
fileChanges.details: The test page needs to be updated via taktician_wp_update to include: (1) DemoContainer wrappers around each variation with demoTitle and demoDescription. (2) Add missing variations: Long Content (post with 100+ char heading and 500+ char description), Empty Optional Fields (post with only blockVariation, no heading/description/buttons), Eyebrow variation (resource with eyebrow text), Single Column variation (post with maxColumns=1). (3) Keep existing 7 variations but wrap them. Total: 11 variations.

designDecisions.question: Blog card hover: should the image zoom use scale(1.05) matching team member card pattern, or a different scale?
designDecisions.recommendation: Use scale(1.05) with transition-transform duration-300 to match the team member card hover pattern referenced in the report. The Figma hover state (811:59803) shows the image with slightly enlarged appearance.

designDecisions.question: Policy card tag pills on hover: should they invert to a darker background (like charcoal/10) or become transparent/outlined?
designDecisions.recommendation: Use group-hover:bg-white/80 group-hover:border-charcoal/20 to match how they appear in the Figma hover state (811:59964) where tags have a lighter appearance on the accent background. Same pattern for resource cards.

designDecisions.question: Blog card: should the Learn More accent color also trigger on focus-within (keyboard navigation) in addition to hover?
designDecisions.recommendation: Yes, use both :hover and :focus-within on the group/link element to ensure keyboard users see the same visual feedback when tabbing to the card link.

designDecisions.question: Focus management after AJAX load: where should focus move after results are replaced?
designDecisions.recommendation: After a filter/search AJAX load (non-append), focus should move to the results container (.archive-results) which has aria-live="polite". After Load More append, focus should remain on the Load More button (or move to the first newly appended card if the button is removed because it was the last page). This follows WAI-ARIA guidance for dynamic content updates.

accessibilityRequirements.summary: The block type doc (post-type-archive.md Section 15) references Landmarks and Button accessibility patterns. Current implementation covers basics but is missing several requirements.

implemented: Filter form has role="form" with aria-label="Archive filters"
Results container has aria-live="polite" for AJAX updates
Filter pill remove buttons have descriptive aria-labels
missing: aria-busy toggling on results container during AJAX loads (required by aria-live best practices)
Focus management after AJAX content replacement (users lose their place)
Load More button: no aria-disabled or loading state announcement when clicked
Loading spinner: no role="status" or aria-label for screen readers
Filter pills: no keyboard event handlers (Enter/Space) -- relies on native button behavior which should work, but verify
Per-page selector: no aria-label describing its purpose
Clear All buttons: missing aria-label (both in-form and in active-filters area)
No skip link or landmark role on the results area for efficient navigation
Cards wrapped in <a> containing <article>: verify this doesn't cause assistive technology confusion (a>article is valid HTML5 but some screen readers may announce oddly)

requiredPatterns.name: Landmarks
keyboardInteractions: No special keyboard interaction -- landmarks enable efficient navigation via screen reader shortcuts
ariaRequirements: Section element provides page section landmark
Filter form with role="form" or role="search" and aria-label
Pagination nav element provides navigation landmark with aria-label
Results container should have role="region" with aria-label when using aria-live

requiredPatterns.name: Button
keyboardInteractions: Space key: activates Load More button
Enter key: activates Load More button
Space key: activates filter pill remove buttons
Enter key: activates filter pill remove buttons
Space key: activates Clear All button
Enter key: activates Clear All button
ariaRequirements: Load More button: aria-disabled="true" when loading, with loading text or aria-label
Filter pill buttons: accessible label computed from text content + remove context

qaChecks.check: Verify aria-busy toggles on .archive-results during AJAX loads
qaChecks.how: Use taktician_page_audit to check for aria-busy attribute, then use taktician_screenshot with actions to trigger a filter change and verify the attribute toggles

qaChecks.check: Verify focus moves to results container after AJAX content replacement (non-append)
qaChecks.how: Use taktician_screenshot with actions to trigger a filter, then check document.activeElement

qaChecks.check: Verify Load More button shows aria-disabled during loading
qaChecks.how: Use taktician_screenshot with actions to click Load More and capture mid-loading state

qaChecks.check: Verify loading spinner has role="status" and aria-label

qaChecks.how: Use taktician_page_audit to inspect select[data-type='per_page'] for aria-label

qaChecks.check: Verify Clear All buttons have accessible labels
qaChecks.how: Use taktician_page_audit to inspect button[data-clear-all] for aria-label

qaChecks.check: Verify all card links have accessible names (not just the URL)
qaChecks.how: Use taktician_page_audit to inspect card link elements for aria-label or meaningful text content

qaChecks.check: Verify keyboard navigation through filter dropdowns, search, per-page, and cards works without traps
qaChecks.how: Use taktician_screenshot with keyboard actions (Tab, Enter, Escape) to navigate through the filter bar

attributes.description: Have a burning question, comment, or concern? Connect with one of our staff members.

variations.demoDescription: Team members grid with department filter, per-page selector, and CTA button. Tests accent-color card backgrounds, photo from meta, name from first_name+last_name.

attributes.heading: We're Here to Help!
attributes.description: Have a burning question, comment, or concern? Connect with one of our staff members. From career success to wellness, we're ready to support you.

attributes.description: Have a burning question, comment, or concern? Connect with one of our staff members. From career success to wellness, we're ready to support you.

attributes.description: You can find a collection of IGNITE's policies and financial statements, as well as a schedule of board meetings for the upcoming year, and executive summaries of previous meetings.

variations.demoDescription: Testimonials without filters, search, or per-page controls. Tests private CPT rendering with no links, blockquote display.
Blog with no filters, no search, no per-page selector. Uses standard pagination mode. Tests minimal/empty configuration.
Blog with all controls enabled and standard pagination (not load-more). Tests Showing X to Y of Z, Prev/Next navigation.
Tests overflow handling with extremely long heading and description text. Verifies text truncation/wrapping behavior.

attributes.eyebrow: This Is An Intentionally Very Long Eyebrow Text To Test Overflow
attributes.heading: This Is An Intentionally Very Long Heading Text That Exceeds One Hundred Characters to Test How the Layout Handles Overflow and Line Wrapping in the Archive Block Heading Area
attributes.description: This is a very long description text that is intended to test how the archive block handles excessive content in the description area. It should gracefully wrap and not break the two-column layout. The description continues with more text to push well past the typical content length. variations.demoDescription: Tests shouldDisplay() behavior: no eyebrow, no heading, no description, no buttons. Only the filter bar and grid should render.
Tests eyebrow text rendering above the heading. Verifies the eyebrow is visible and properly styled in dark mode.
Tests maxColumns=1 configuration. All cards should be full-width stacked vertically.

interactiveTests.name: Resource card hover shows accent background
interactiveTests.expect: Card background transitions to accent color, text becomes charcoal, tags remain visible with inverted styling, VIEW RESOURCE text becomes charcoal

interactiveTests.name: Policy card hover shows accent fill
interactiveTests.expect: Entire card fills with accent color, tags visible, title becomes charcoal, VIEW RESOURCE text becomes charcoal

interactiveTests.name: Blog card hover triggers image zoom AND Learn More accent color simultaneously
interactiveTests.expect: Post thumbnail image scales up (1.05x) AND Learn More text changes to accent color -- both triggered by hovering the parent link element

interactiveTests.name: Load More button appends results
interactiveTests.expect: Additional cards are appended to the grid, Load More button updates or disappears if last page

interactiveTests.name: Filter dropdown adds active filter pill
interactiveTests.expect: Filter pill appears in active filters area, results update via AJAX

interactiveTests.name: Team member card photo scales on hover
interactiveTests.expect: Team member photo image scales up slightly (1.05x) with smooth transition

output.qaInstructions: Full QA required after changes. QA should specifically verify: (1) Resource and policy card hover effects including tag visibility, btn-tertiary color change, and smooth transitions. (2) Blog card simultaneous image zoom + Learn More accent color on parent link hover. (3) All accessibility additions (aria-busy, focus management, aria-labels on controls, loading spinner role). (4) Test page renders correctly with DemoContainer wrappers and all 11 variations. (5) Run interactive tests for hover states and AJAX filtering. (6) Run taktician_a11y_test for landmarks and button patterns.
